$comment: Seed prompts for e2e_benchmark.py. Categorized by which subsystem we expect to handle each. Order matters — earlier prompts grow the registry that later prompts depend on. The runner records the actual outcome; the verified subset becomes benchmark.json.

prompts.prompt: What is the capital of Brazil?
What is the capital of Mongolia?
What is the capital of Kazakhstan?
How many people live in Lagos, Nigeria?
What is the population of Iceland?
What is the population of Singapore?
Who is the current CEO of Anthropic?
Who founded Linux?
Who is the author of The Pragmatic Programmer?
What year was Python 3.0 released?
What year did Tim Berners-Lee invent the World Wide Web?
What year was the Linux kernel first released?
When did the Hubble Space Telescope launch?
What is the boiling point of mercury in Celsius?
What is the speed of sound in dry air at 20°C?

What is Avogadro's number?

What is an SBOM in software supply chain security?

What is the difference between TCP and UDP?
What is the difference between SQL and NoSQL databases?

How do I count lines in a file using wc?
What is one notable AI announcement from earlier this month?
What is the latest stable Python version?
What is the latest LTS version of Node.js?
What is the latest version of PostgreSQL?

How many kilometers in 100 nautical miles?
Convert 32 fluid ounces to milliliters.

Which mountain range separates Europe and Asia?
What is the longest river in South America?

What is mitosis, in one sentence?
Give me a one-paragraph summary of CRISPR.
Fetch https://example.com and tell me the page title.
Fetch https://www.iana.org/help/example-domains and summarize in two sentences.
List the outbound links on https://example.com.
Fetch the RSS feed at https://hnrss.org/frontpage and report 5 titles.
Fetch https://blog.python.org/feeds/posts/default and list the latest 3 posts.
Use check_python_syntax on: def add(a, b): return a + b
Use check_python_syntax on: def broken(a, b): return a +

Use extract_code_metrics on: def foo():
    if 1: return 1
    if 2: return 2
    return 3

Use extract_code_metrics on: class A:
    def m(self): pass
    def n(self): pass
Use detect_language on: console.log("hello world");
Use detect_language on: import numpy as np; print(np.linalg.norm([3,4]))
Use detect_language on: fn main() { println!("{}", 1+1); }
Use detect_language on: package main; import "fmt"; func main(){fmt.Println(1)}
Use detect_language on: SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE active = 1;

Use detect_language on: services:
  web:
    image: nginx:latest
Use get_system_metrics to report current CPU and memory.
Use get_system_metrics_with_alerts and just show the alerts list.

Use list_top_processes with by=memory, n=5.
Use list_top_processes with by=cpu, n=10.
Use check_disk_usage on path "/".
Use check_disk_usage on path "/tmp".
Use find_large_files on path "/tmp" with min_size_mb=1, top_n=5.
Use find_large_files on path "/var" with min_size_mb=10, top_n=5.
Use get_service_status with service="sshd".
Use get_service_status with service="cron".
